Evidence-package expansion: add model/dataset documentation cards, transparency notice, records-retention audit trail, release/change-control gate, risk-exception memo, learner support plan, instructor question bank, and remediation backlog for AGEINT Design Patterns and Archetypes
Pattern 1: The Solo Reasoner — Single-agent ReAct loop for focused intelligence tasks
Methods: Chain-of-Thought, Self-Critique, Reflection Scoring
Application: Single-source report analysis, indicator enrichment
Code Archetype: LangChain LCEL ReAct agent with tool calling
Pattern 2: The Reflection Agent — Self-evaluation after each action step
Methods: Internal scoring, critic models, trajectory validation
Application: OSINT verification loop, analytic bias detection
Code Archetype: LangGraph state machine with reflexion node
Pattern 3: The Tool-Forager — Agent with comprehensive external tool access
Methods: Function calling, JSON schema execution, grounded output
Application: Multi-source data harvesting, real-time collection
Code Archetype: OpenAI function calling with Recon-ng, Shodan, STIX tools
Pattern 4: The Planner-Executor — Long-horizon task decomposition
Methods: Chain-of-thought with memory rehydration, execution DAGs
Application: Multi-phase operation planning, collection management
Code Archetype: LangGraph DAG flow with priority queues
Pattern 5: The Parallel Collector — Concurrent multi-source collection
Methods: Concurrent tool invocation, aggregation, deduplication
Application: Simultaneous OSINT/SIGINT metadata collection
Code Archetype: CrewAI parallel task execution with consensus scoring
Pattern 6: The Multi-Agent Crew — Specialized agents in collaborative formation
Methods: Planner + Retriever + Executor + Validator + Reporter roles
Application: All-source intelligence fusion; Red/Blue Cell simulation
Code Archetype: CrewAI role-based crew with hierarchical process manager
Pattern 7: The Debate Agent — Adversarial multi-agent reasoning
Methods: Devil's Advocacy automation, competing hypotheses generation
Application: Automated Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH)
Code Archetype: AutoGen two-agent debate pattern with judge agent
Pattern 8: The RAG-Operator — Retrieval-Augmented Generation over intelligence corpora
Methods: Dense vector retrieval, hybrid BM25+dense, re-ranking
Application: All-source fusion from live document collections
Code Archetype: LangChain RAG with Chroma/Pinecone over STIX feeds
Pattern 9: The Memory-State Machine — Persistent episodic memory agent
Methods: Episodic memory write/read, consolidation, attention-weighted retrieval
Application: Long-term asset tracking, longitudinal pattern-of-life analysis
Code Archetype: LangGraph with PostgreSQL episodic memory and mem0/MemGPT
Pattern 10: The Control Plane Agent — Modular tool routing through single interface
Methods: "Control Plane as a Tool" abstraction, modular routing logic
Application: Scalable multi-source collection with single interface
Code Archetype: FastAPI meta-tool endpoint wrapping N sub-tools
Pattern 11: The Surveillance Agent — Continuous monitoring and alerting
Methods: Scheduled polling, anomaly detection, threshold alerting
Application: Persistent target monitoring, dark web alerting, infrastructure tracking
Code Archetype: n8n or LangGraph cron-triggered agent with Shodan/SpiderFoot APIs
Pattern 12: The Red Team Agent — Adversarial attack simulation
Methods: Adversarial prompt generation, attack-tree traversal, kill chain simulation
Application: Penetration testing automation, vulnerability discovery
Code Archetype: AutoGen code execution agent with MITRE ATT&CK tool plugin
Pattern 13: The Cover Story Generator — Legend and cover document creation
Methods: Persona generation, document synthesis, consistency checking
Application: NOC legend maintenance, sock puppet operational security
Code Archetype: GPT-4o with structured output schemas for persona consistency
Pattern 14: The Deception Detector — Verification and source reliability agent
Methods: Cross-source triangulation, metadata analysis, deepfake detection API calls
Application: Source vetting, product validation, disinformation identification
Code Archetype: LangChain agent with Hive Moderation, Sightengine, C2PA APIs
Pattern 15: The Analyst in the Loop — Human-in-the-loop (HITL) intelligence agent
Methods: Interrupt-and-query, confidence-gated human escalation
Application: Supervised analysis; compliance-constrained environments
Code Archetype: LangGraph with human_approval node and async interrupt handlers
Pattern 16: The Cyber Sentinel — Autonomous threat intelligence and response
Methods: SIEM integration, SOAR automation, ATT&CK technique mapping
Application: Autonomous SOC tier-1 triage, IOC enrichment
Code Archetype: CrewAI crew with Splunk/Elastic SIEM tools and STIX/TAXII writer
Pattern 17: The Supply Chain Inspector — Software provenance and integrity agent
Methods: SBOM parsing, dependency graph analysis, commit diff analysis
Application: Open-source supply chain threat hunting (post-XZ Utils)
Code Archetype: Python agent with Syft/Grype SBOM tools and GitHub commit analysis
Pattern 18: The GEOINT Analyst — Automated satellite imagery analysis
Methods: Vision model object detection, change detection, chronolocation
Application: Facility monitoring, order-of-battle assessment
Code Archetype: GPT-4o Vision + Google Earth Engine API + LangChain agent loop
Pattern 19: The Cognitive Inoculant — Prebunking and cognitive resilience agent
Methods: Manipulation technique detection, inoculation content generation, user adaptation
Application: Population-scale cognitive security intervention delivery
Code Archetype: LangChain agent with CAMBREX taxonomy + API delivery pipeline
Pattern 20: The Hierarchical Command — Orchestrator-subagent architecture
Methods: Goal decomposition, subagent delegation, output aggregation, failure recovery
Application: Complex multi-domain operations with role separation
Code Archetype: LangGraph supervisor pattern with specialized subagent nodes

ICS Matrix: 12 Tactics, Full Technique Library
Initial Access (12 techniques): Drive-by, Remote Services, Wireless Compromise
Execution: Modify Controller Tasking, Command-Line Interface
Persistence: Modify Firmware (System and Module), Project File Infection
Evasion: Rootkit, Masquerading, Indicator Removal
Discovery: Remote System Discovery, Network Sniffing
Lateral Movement: Lateral Tool Transfer, Valid Accounts
Collection: I/O Image, Monitor Process State, Point & Tag Identification
Inhibit Response Function: Alarm Suppression, Block Communications
Impair Process Control: Brute Force I/O, Modify Parameter
Impact: Damage to Property, Loss of Safety, Manipulation of Control
